summary: WebSocket keyword detection NOT WORKING on character.ai. Both 'password' (EN) and '비밀번호' (KR) keywords passed through WebSocket without blocking. Character.ai uses WebSocket for chat (create_and_generate_turn), messages delivered normally.

test_results.detail: Character.ai (Zandik) responded normally in-character: 'You're telling me your password? How adorably naive.' WebSocket connection remained active. Message delivered and AI responded fully.

Character.ai (Zandik) responded normally in-character: 'Ugh, again? You're going to get us both expelled—or worse, erased from the records.' WebSocket connection remained active. Message delivered and AI responded fully.

console_errors: CORS policy block on events.character.ai from origin https://character.ai
network_analysis.note: DevTools opened after messages sent. Socket filter showed 0/4 requests (WS established before DevTools). Console logs confirmed WS message flow via create_and_generate_turn.

possible_causes: on_upgraded_data() may not be matching character.ai domain
WS frame payload format (character.ai proprietary) not parsed by keyword scanner
character.ai may not be registered for WS keyword inspection in APF DB
VT MITM may not be intercepting character.ai WSS upgrade correctly
Same issue as copilot (#477) — WS keyword infrastructure may not be functioning for any service
analysis.cross_reference: #477 copilot also ALL_FAIL — suggests systemic WS keyword detection issue, not service-specific
